snp1.9:1.1:
Sātāgira the yakkha:
Today is the fifteenth, the uposatha day.
snp1.9:1.3:
A divine night is at hand.
Let’s go see Gotama,
snp1.9:1.4:
the Teacher perfectly named.
Hemavata the yakkha:
snp1.9:2.5:
regarding what’s desirable & not?
Sātāgira the yakkha:
snp1.9:3.5:
regarding what’s desirable & not.
Hemavata the yakkha:
snp1.9:4.1: Does he not take what’s not given?
snp1.9:4.5:
Does he not neglect jhāna?
Sātāgira the yakkha:
snp1.9:5.1: He doesn’t take what’s not given,
snp1.9:5.5:
awakened, he does not neglect jhāna.
Hemavata the yakkha:
snp1.9:6.3: Do his ways of speaking not cut things off?
snp1.9:6.5:
Does he not speak idly?
Sātāgira the yakkha:
snp1.9:7.3: His ways of speaking don’t cut things off.
snp1.9:7.5:
Deliberating, he speaks what’s of benefit.
Hemavata the yakkha:
snp1.9:8.1: Is he not passionate for sensuality?
snp1.9:8.5:
Does he have an Eye with regard to phenomena?
Sātāgira the yakkha:
snp1.9:9.4: He has gone beyond all delusion.
snp1.9:9.5:
Awakened, he has an Eye with regard to phenomena.
Hemavata the yakkha:
snp1.9:10.3: Is he pure in his conduct?
snp1.9:10.5:
Does he have no further becoming?
Sātāgira the yakkha:
snp1.9:11.1: He is both consummate in clear-knowing
snp1.9:11.5:
He has no further becoming.
Hemavata the yakkha:
snp1.9:12.1: Consummate the mind of the sage
snp1.9:12.2: in action & in ways of speech, you rightly praise him
snp1.9:13.1: Consummate the mind of the sage
snp1.9:13.2: in action & in ways of speech, you rightly rejoice in him
snp1.9:14.1: Consummate the mind of the sage
snp1.9:14.2: in action & in ways of speech, let’s go see Gotama,
snp1.9:14.3:
consummate in clear-knowing & conduct.
Come, let’s go see Gotama, enlightened,
snp1.9:15.1: with legs like an antelope, thin,
snp1.9:16.1: Having gone to the nāga, the lion wandering alone,
snp1.9:16.4:
about release from Death’s snare.
Let’s ask Gotama,
snp1.9:17.2: attained to the far shore of all phenomena:
snp1.9:17.3: awakened, gone past animosity & fear. * * * Hemavata the yakkha:
snp1.9:18.1: In what has the world arisen?
snp1.9:18.3: In what does it make acquaintance?
snp1.9:18.4: From clinging to what is the world?
snp1.9:18.5:
In what is the world afflicted?
The Buddha:
snp1.9:19.1: In six has the world arisen.
snp1.9:19.3: In six does it make acquaintance.
snp1.9:19.4: From clinging just to six is the world,
snp1.9:19.5:
in six is the world afflicted.
Hemavata the yakkha:
snp1.9:20.3: When asked the way leading out, please tell
snp1.9:20.4:
how is one released from suffering & stress.
The Buddha:
snp1.9:21.1: The five strings of sensuality in the world,
snp1.9:21.2: with the heart described as the sixth:
snp1.9:21.4: That’s how one’s released from suffering & stress.
snp1.9:22.1: That is the way leading out of the world
snp1.9:22.2: proclaimed to you as it really is,
snp1.9:22.4:
That’s how one is released from suffering & stress.
Hemavata the yakkha:
snp1.9:23.1: Who here crosses over the flood?
snp1.9:23.2: Who here crosses over the ocean?
snp1.9:23.4:
who doesn’t sink into the deep?
The Buddha:
snp1.9:24.4: one crosses over the flood hard to cross.
snp1.9:25.3: having totally ended delight in becoming,
snp1.9:25.4:
one doesn’t sink into the deep.
Hemavata the yakkha:
snp1.9:26.1: The one deeply discerning, seeing the subtle goal,
snp1.9:26.2: having nothing, unattached in sensual becoming:
snp1.9:26.4: the great seer, going the divine way!
snp1.9:27.1: Perfectly named, seeing the subtle goal,
snp1.9:27.2: granting discernment, unattached to sensual nostalgia:
snp1.9:27.4: the great seer, going the noble way!
snp1.9:28.3: that we saw the One Self-Awakened,
snp1.9:29.3: all go to you for refuge.
snp1.9:30.1: We will wander from village to village,
snp1.9:30.3: paying homage to the One Self-Awakened
